title: echinospartum boissieri subsp. webbii
intro: Echinospartum boissieri subsp. webbii, also known as Webb's spine-flower, is a plant species within the Fabaceae family, characterized by its shrub-like growth habit and small, yellowish flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also believed to have health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and acting as an antiseptic agent. Echinospartum boissieri subsp. webbii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
